title: Fix persistent Google access/OAuth issue

requirements.text: Investigate and fix the Google access issue that persists after Version 9. The backend currently makes HTTP outcalls — ensure the search backend is fully migrated away from any Google endpoints to DuckDuckGo, with no remaining references to Google APIs, OAuth, or Google domains in the Motoko actor or frontend hooks.

quotes: The access issue is not gone

acceptanceCriteria: No HTTP outcalls to any Google domain (google.com, googleapis.com, etc.) remain in the backend actor
All search queries are routed exclusively to the DuckDuckGo API endpoint
Search results load successfully without any access-denied or OAuth errors

requirements.text: Audit the frontend search hook (useSearch.ts) and SearchInterface component to ensure no Google-specific URLs, API keys, or OAuth flows are referenced. Remove or replace any remaining Google references.

quotes: The access issue is not gone

acceptanceCriteria: No Google API URLs or OAuth references exist in frontend source files
Search results render correctly from DuckDuckGo responses
No access-denied error messages appear in the search UI

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in a single actor in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or layout of the search UI
Adding new search providers beyond DuckDuckGo
Modifying authentication or Internet Identity flows
